summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held a regular meeting featuring extensive ceremonial swearing-in of new firefighters, police officers, and promotions, as well as multiple life-saving awards presented to police and fire personnel. The board approved a guaranteed maximum price construction agreement with Conger Construction for Fire Station 18, which is under budget with no local levy tax dollars being used. Other business included hiring a part-time firefighter EMT, accepting a detective's resignation to part-time status, approving travel to the Ohio Township Association Winter Conference, and supporting the annual Light Up Goshen parade.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (October 10–22, 2024) – passed
Motion to enter into GMP agreement with Conger Construction for Station 18 construction – passed
Motion to hire part-time firefighter EMT Tyler Harding at $16/hour effective November 3, 2024 – passed
Motion to accept full-time resignation of Detective Steve Maynard effective January 3, 2025 and appoint him to part-time police detective position – passed
Motion to approve out-of-town travel for OTA Winter Conference (1/29/25–1/31/25 in Columbus) – passed
Motion to authorize township resources for Light Up Goshen parade on November 23 – passed
Motion to adjourn into executive session at 7:29 PM per ORC 121.22G1 – passed
Motion to reappoint Dave Curie to Zoning Commission with term expiring December 31, 2029 – passed
Motion to reappoint Gary Theodore to Board of Zoning Appeals – passed
Motion to suspend full-time firefighter paramedic Gabe Grooms for two 24-hour shift days (48 hours) without pay per CBA Article 25 – passed
action_items: HR Eckenhorst to finalize supplemental benefit package for the board, possibly by next meeting
Administrator Rose to schedule OTA Winter Conference travel for seven or more staff and elected officials
Police department to coordinate traffic management and vehicle participation for Light Up Goshen parade on November 23
Determine Detective Maynard's part-time wage when 2025 non-union salaries are approved
key_amounts: $2.5 million from state capital fund for Station 18 construction
75% of remaining Station 18 costs covered by Ohio Emergency Management Agency
No local levy tax dollars used for Station 18 construction
$16/hour wage for part-time firefighter EMT Tyler Harding
48 hours without pay suspension for firefighter paramedic Gabe Grooms
105 hours worked by staff during Homorama event
notable_topics: Swearing-in of new firefighters: Alyssa Ladd, Shailer Newman, Carly Hargis, Jack Pegram
Promotion of Dustin Englund to Lieutenant, John Fiela to Captain, Kevin Lynch to Deputy Chief
Swearing-in of new police officers Gary Moen and Wesley Lyons
Promotion of Kyle Howell to Police Sergeant
Life-saving awards for Officers Tyler Smith and Jamie McFarland (armed man de-escalation incident)
Life-saving award for Sergeant Cody Collier (motorcycle crash tourniquet application on SR 28)
Life-saving awards for multi-agency team response to cardiac event on Woodville Pike
Certificate of Appreciation for Chaplain Aaron Ellermann
Fire Station 18 construction update – under budget, groundbreaking imminent
GMP agreement with Conger Construction for Station 18
Detective Steve Maynard transitioning to part-time retirement role
Ohio Township Association Winter Conference travel approval
Zoning Commission and Board of Zoning Appeals reappointments
Disciplinary action against firefighter paramedic Gabe Grooms
notes: Trustee Hausermann was absent due to his grandson's wedding in Florida. The meeting featured an unusually large audience for the ceremonial portions. The transcript contains significant repeated filler text ('Board of Trustees Meeting. Clermont County, Ohio 45122. Roads.') likely due to automated transcription errors. A recess was taken at approximately 7:23 PM for refreshments provided by the CPAAA. Station 18 construction is fully funded without local levy tax dollars, using insurance funds, state capital funds ($2.5M), and 75% from Ohio Emergency Management Agency.
title: BOT 10-22-24
